2604.24096.title: Meta-Ensemble Learning with Diverse Data Splits for Improved Respiratory Sound Classification
2604.24096.paper_summary: Training reliable respiratory sound classification models remains challenging due to the limited size and subject diversity of datasets. Ensemble methods can improve robustness, but when base models are trained on identical data, models tend to overfit and produce highly correlated predictions, thereby reducing the effectiveness of ensembling. In this work, we investigate a meta-ensemble learning methodology that enhances prediction diversity by training base models on diverse data splits and combining their outputs through a trained meta-model. Specifically, we train base models on the ICBHI dataset using two data split settings: fixed 80-20% split and five-fold cross-validation split, under two data granularity settings: patient- and sample-level. The resulting diversity in base model predictions enables the meta-model to better generalize. Our approach achieves new state-of-the-art performance on the ICBHI benchmark, reaching a Score of 66.49% and showing improved generalization on two out-of-distribution datasets, indicating its potential applicability to real-world clinical data.
2604.24096.paper_summary_zh: 訓練可靠的呼吸聲音分類模型仍然具有挑戰性，因為數據集的大小和受試者多樣性有限。<lb/>集成方法可以提高穩健性，但當基礎模型在相同數據上訓練時，模型往往會過擬合並產生高度相關的預測，從而降低集成的有效性。<lb/>在本研究中，我們探討了一種元集成學習方法，通過在多樣化的數據劃分上訓練基礎模型並通過訓練的元模型結合其輸出來增強預測多樣性。<lb/>具體而言，我們在ICBHI數據集上使用兩種數據劃分設置訓練基礎模型：固定的80-20%劃分和五折交叉驗證劃分，並在兩種數據粒度設置下進行：患者級和樣本級。<lb/>基礎模型預測中的多樣性使得元模型能夠更好地進行泛化。<lb/>我們的方法在ICBHI基準上達到了新的最先進性能，得分為66.49%，並在兩個分佈外數據集上顯示出改進的泛化能力，表明其在現實臨床數據中的潛在應用性。
2604.24096.author: June-Woo Kim et.al.
2604.24096.authors: June-Woo Kim, Miika Toikkanen, Heejoon Koo, Yoon Tae Kim, Doyoung Kwon, Kyunghoon Kim
